titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD 1.4.Ebor.10

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=334 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:44:43.39763+00 | translation moa=325 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:44:43.387483+00

p.cl:
. Ric’ dei gratia ecclesie sancti Andr’ humilis minister  omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis tocius
sue diocesis . salutem .

Notum sit dilectioni uestre controuersiam que fuit inter ecclesiam
Dunolmensem et ecclesiam croilandensem terminatam esse apud striuelin in presencia
Willelmi Regis Scottorum et nostra et multorum aliorum proborum uirorum tam clericorum
quam laicorum . ita scilicet  quod villa de ederham et ecclesia eiusdem ville
cum omnibus capellis et pertinenciis suis cessit in perpetuum ecclesie dunolmensi  ces
sante omni reclamatione croilandensis ecclesie de prenominata possessione .

Testibus. Engelramo episcopo Glasguensi . Gaufrido abbate dunfermeliensi . Willelmo abbate
de sancta cruce . aluredo abbate de striuelin . Waltero priore de sancto andrea . Nicolao
cancellario . Andrea et Matheo archidiaconis . Aiulfo et Iocio decanis . Roberto fratre episcopi . Nigello
capellannus . Roberto canonico de sancto Andrea . Roberto filio Saulfi . Magistro Herberto . Alexandro capellano
Richard by the grace of God humble servant of the church of St Andrews to all sons of holy Mother Church of his entire diocese greeting.

Be it known to your love that the dispute which existed between the church of Durham and the church of Croyland has been concluded at Stirling in the presence of William king of the Scots and our own and of many other worthy men as well cleric as lay, so that the toun of Edrom and the church of the same toun with all its chapels and pertinents falls in perpetuity to the church of Durham, with all counter-claiming in respect of the aforenamed property by the church of Croyland ceasing.

As witnesses: Ingram, bishop of Glasgow; Geoffrey, abbot of Dunfermline; William, abbot of Holyrood; Ailred, abbot of Stirling; Walter, prior of St Andrews; Nicholas, chancellor; Andrew and Matthew, archdeacons; Aiulf and Iocius, deans; Robert the bishop's brother; Nigel the chaplain; Robert, canon of St Andrews; Robert son of Saulf; Master Herbert; Alexander the chaplain.
